name: Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies
description: A volunteer organization of scientists, engineers, and academics who apply rigorous scientific methodology to the analysis of UAP reports. Best known for the 270-page forensic analysis of the 2004 Nimitz incident and the peer-reviewed Entropy paper on Tic Tac flight characteristics by Kevin Knuth. Publishes technical reports and hosts an annual conference.

name: SOL Foundation
description: A research institute founded to support rigorous scientific and policy investigation of UAP, led by Garry Nolan and Peter Skafish. Hosts an annual symposium at Stanford University featuring scientists, policy professionals, and researchers. Distinguished by its institutional affiliation with Stanford and its emphasis on consciousness research alongside physical analysis.

name: Galileo Project
description: Established by Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb following his Oumuamua hypothesis. Operates a multi-sensor observatory in the Harvard Yard to detect and characterize anomalous aerial objects in real-time. Uses a telescope array, infrared cameras, radio receivers, and audio sensors. Takes a purely empirical observational approach distinct from report-analysis models.

name: Bigelow Institute for Consciousness Studies
description: Founded by Robert Bigelow following his sale of Bigelow Aerospace. BICS funds research into consciousness survival after death and anomalous cognition, areas with significant overlap with UAP contact phenomenology. Known for hosting essay competitions with $1M+ in prizes and funding independent researchers globally.

name: J. Allen Hynek Center for UFO Studies
description: Founded by J. Allen Hynek after his break with Project Blue Book. Maintains the UFOCAT cross-referenced database (one of the five sources in DECUR's sightings integration) and publishes the International UFO Reporter. The oldest continuously operating scientific UAP research organization in the United States.

name: National Aviation Reporting Center on Anomalous Phenomena
description: A research organization focused specifically on UAP encounters in commercial and military aviation. Collects reports from pilots and air traffic controllers who cannot file formal reports without career risk. Maintains a database of aviation-specific UAP encounters with safety implications. Works with ICAO on reporting protocol development.

name: Mutual UFO Network
description: The largest civilian UAP investigation organization in the world. Operates a network of trained field investigators who respond to reports, conduct witness interviews, and file structured investigation reports. MUFON reports form one of the five source databases in DECUR's sightings integration. Has investigated hundreds of thousands of cases since 1969.

name: To The Stars Academy of Arts and Science
description: Founded by Tom DeLonge with a board including Luis Elizondo, Hal Puthoff, and Chris Mellon. Responsible for the declassification and release of the Nimitz, Gimbal, and Go Fast videos in 2017-2018. Also produced the History Channel series 'Unidentified.' Effectively catalyzed the current era of government UAP disclosure through strategic media engagement.

name: UAPx
description: An instrumentally-focused research organization that deploys multi-sensor arrays to field locations with high UAP activity, including the ocean near Puerto Rico. Led in part by Tim Gallaudet. Uses calibrated, timestamped sensor data to capture and characterize anomalous events rather than relying on single-sensor or eyewitness testimony alone.

name: Groupe d'Etudes et d'Informations sur les Phenomenes Aerospatiaux Non-identifies
description: The official French government UAP investigation unit, operated under CNES (the French space agency). One of the oldest continuously operating official UAP investigation programs in the world. Publishes its case database publicly. Categorizes reports into four types (A through D) based on explanatory confidence. Highly regarded for transparency and methodological rigor.

name: Comite de Estudios de Fenomenos Aereos Anomalos
description: Chile's official UAP investigation committee, operating under the Directorate General of Civil Aeronautics. Investigates reports from pilots, air traffic controllers, and military personnel. Released the El Bosque Air Base video in 2014 after a 2-year forensic analysis - one of the most transparent official UAP case releases by any government.

name: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office
description: The U.S. Department of Defense's official UAP investigation office, established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2022. Succeeded the UAP Task Force. Responsible for detecting, identifying, and attributing UAP encounters across all domains - air, space, sea, and subsurface. Maintains a secure reporting mechanism for military and intelligence personnel.

name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: NASA established a UAP research function in 2023 following the UAP Independent Study Team report. Appointed Mark McInerney as Director of UAP Research. Focused on applying NASA's existing sensor assets and data science capabilities to UAP detection and characterization. Represents the first time NASA has formally engaged with the UAP topic institutionally.

name: UAP Disclosure Fund
description: A non-profit advocacy organization that funds legal representation for whistleblowers and lobbies Congress for stronger UAP transparency legislation. Supported the legal proceedings around David Grusch's whistleblower case. Works with the attorneys involved in the National Security Act whistleblower protections that enabled congressional UAP testimony.

name: The Black Vault
description: The largest online FOIA document archive, operated by John Greenewald Jr. since 1996. Has filed over 10,000 FOIA requests and hosts hundreds of thousands of declassified government documents. An essential primary source repository for UAP researchers working with declassified materials. Responsible for obtaining the full AATIP program documents and other key releases.

name: Division of Perceptual Studies
description: The most academically credentialed consciousness research unit in the world, housed within the University of Virginia School of Medicine. Founded by Ian Stevenson, whose 40-year investigation of children's past-life memories produced over 3,000 documented cases. Current leadership under Jim Tucker (reincarnation cases) and Bruce Greyson (near-death experiences) continues peer-reviewed publication in mainstream medical journals. DOPS's methodology - rigorous case collection, blind follow-up, prospective criteria - sets the evidentiary standard for post-mortem consciousness research globally.

name: Parnia Lab
location: NYU Langone Health, New York City, New York
description: Led by Dr. Sam Parnia at NYU Langone, the Parnia Lab runs the largest prospective studies of near-death experiences during cardiac arrest, including the landmark AWARE (AWAreness during REsuscitation) studies. The lab uses calibrated visual targets positioned above resuscitation tables to test out-of-body perception claims under controlled hospital conditions. Parnia's research represents the most methodologically rigorous attempt to empirically test consciousness survival hypotheses in a clinical setting.

name: The Monroe Institute
description: Founded by Robert Monroe, whose 1971 book Journeys Out of the Body documented his systematic out-of-body experiences. The Institute developed Hemi-Sync binaural beat audio technology to induce altered states reproducibly. Gained national security relevance through the CIA's Gateway Process program (declassified 1983), which used Monroe's techniques for remote viewing training. Several AATIP-adjacent figures including Hal Puthoff have professional overlap with Monroe's research lineage via SRI's consciousness studies. The Institute continues residential training programs and research into binaural audio effects on consciousness.

name: SETI Institute
description: The leading scientific institution for the Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence. Operates the Allen Telescope Array and conducts radio and optical SETI surveys across the electromagnetic spectrum. Since 2021, the Institute has increasingly engaged with UAP as a complementary technosignature research domain, arguing that UAP and SETI represent two ends of the same empirical question. SETI researchers Nathalie Cabrol and others have published on the intersection of astrobiology, technosignature detection, and UAP methodology. The Institute receives funding from NASA and private donors including Paul Allen.

name: Essentia Foundation
description: A non-profit foundation established by philosopher and computer scientist Bernardo Kastrup to promote non-materialist interpretations of science and consciousness. Publishes essays, interviews, and original research arguing that consciousness is ontologically fundamental rather than a product of brain chemistry - a framework Kastrup calls 'analytic idealism.' Relevant to UAP research as consciousness-first ontologies are increasingly invoked to account for contact phenomenology, NDE data, and the observer-dependent properties reported in close encounters. Kastrup engages directly with UAP phenomenology in several publications.

name: Society for UAP Studies
description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded by philosopher Michael Cifone (CUNY) to establish UAP Studies as a recognized academic discipline. Publishes Limina: The Journal of UAP Studies, the first peer-reviewed academic journal dedicated exclusively to UAP research. Organizes interdisciplinary working groups spanning physics, philosophy, psychology, and policy. Distinct from case-investigation organizations - SUAPS focuses on discipline-building: developing shared methodological standards, peer-review infrastructure, and academic legitimacy for UAP research.

name: Americans for Safe Aerospace
description: Founded by former U.S. Navy F/A-18 pilot Ryan Graves, ASA advocates for pilot safety and transparent UAP reporting infrastructure in commercial and military aviation. Graves founded the organization following his congressional testimony about persistent UAP encounters by Navy pilots who faced career risk for filing formal reports. ASA works with Congress to establish formal, non-stigmatizing reporting channels and has testified before multiple congressional committees on UAP aviation safety. One of the most credible post-2023 UAP advocacy organizations.

name: Society for Scientific Exploration
description: An international scientific organization of researchers and academics dedicated to rigorous study of anomalous phenomena at the frontiers of science. The SSE publishes the Journal of Scientific Exploration (JSE), one of the few peer-reviewed journals that accepts UAP, parapsychology, and consciousness research. Members include fellows Hal Puthoff and Jacques Vallee. Holds an annual conference that bridges anomalistics, parapsychology, and UAP research in an academic setting. The SSE's peer-review infrastructure has been a critical credibility anchor for the broader anomalistics field.

name: Parapsychological Association
description: The international professional organization of scientists and scholars engaged in the study of psi phenomena - anomalous processes of information and energy transfer not explicable within the current scientific consensus. Founded in 1957, the PA was elected an affiliate of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) in 1969, giving it unusual institutional standing for a parapsychology body. Members publish in peer-reviewed journals, the PA hosts an annual international conference, and it awards research grants to early-career investigators. Hal Puthoff is among the figures associated with the PA's research lineage.

name: Society for Psychical Research
description: The oldest scientific organization dedicated to the investigation of psychic phenomena, founded by Cambridge University scholars in 1882. The SPR has produced rigorous investigations of telepathy, apparitions, near-death experiences, and anomalous cognition throughout its history. Publishes the Journal of the Society for Psychical Research and maintains the free Psi Encyclopedia as an online reference resource. Its membership has included Nobel laureates and professors of psychology and philosophy. The SPR's methodological standards were foundational to modern parapsychology and remain among the most rigorous in the field.

name: Rhine Research Center
description: The oldest continuously operating parapsychology research institution in the United States, tracing its origin to J.B. Rhine's foundational ESP experiments at Duke University in 1935. Rhine's statistical card-guessing experiments established the modern scientific methodology for psi research and coined the term 'extrasensory perception.' The Center continues experimental research on ESP, psychokinesis, and anomalous cognition, and publishes the Journal of Parapsychology. An essential historical and ongoing institutional anchor of the parapsychology field.

name: International Association for Near-Death Studies
description: The leading international organization for near-death experience research and experiencer support, founded by researcher Kenneth Ring and physician Michael Sabom. IANDS publishes the Journal of Near-Death Studies - the only peer-reviewed academic journal dedicated to NDE research - and hosts an annual conference. The organization maintains strong connections with the Division of Perceptual Studies at UVA and the Parnia Lab at NYU, which conduct the most rigorous clinical NDE research. Experiencer testimony from NDE cases has growing methodological and phenomenological overlap with UAP contact research.

name: International Remote Viewing Association
description: The professional organization for remote viewing research, training, and application, founded to preserve and advance the methodology developed at SRI International under CIA funding in the 1970s-90s. The SRI remote viewing program, run in part by Hal Puthoff and involving figures including Ingo Swann and Pat Price, is one of the most extensively documented government-funded psi research programs in history. IRVA publishes Aperture magazine, hosts an annual conference, and maintains standards for controlled remote viewing methodology. The connection between remote viewing research and UAP investigation - involving anomalous perception, consciousness, and intelligence community interest - is a significant thread in DECUR's coverage.

name: Project Hessdalen
description: A long-running scientific monitoring project studying recurring unexplained light phenomena in the Hessdalen Valley of Norway. Since the early 1980s, local residents and researchers have documented anomalous luminous objects of varying sizes, shapes, and behaviors that cannot be attributed to known natural or artificial sources. Established by physicist Erling Strand, the project has involved collaboration with Norwegian universities, the Italian CNR, and international researchers. It maintains a permanent automated monitoring station with magnetometers, spectral analyzers, and cameras. Hessdalen is among the most rigorously instrumented long-term UAP-adjacent field studies in existence, with over four decades of continuous data.

name: Foundation for Research into Extraterrestrial and Extraordinary Experiences
description: A non-profit research organization founded by astronaut Edgar Mitchell and astrophysicist Rudy Schild to conduct systematic academic research on UAP contact and extraordinary experiencer phenomena. FREE's multi-phase survey collected data from over 4,000 respondents who reported UAP contact or other anomalous experiences, producing the most comprehensive dataset on experiencer phenomenology yet published. The survey results challenge conventional assumptions about contact experiences and have been cited in academic literature on consciousness and UAP research. FREE collaborates with researchers at the intersection of UAP, consciousness, and anomalous experience.

name: Declassify UAP
description: A U.S.-based advocacy organization focused on legislative and regulatory reform to require disclosure of government-held UAP information. Works to build public and congressional support for stronger transparency requirements in the UAP domain, with particular focus on NDAA UAP provisions and their implementation by AARO and the intelligence community. Operates in a complementary space to the UAP Disclosure Fund, which focuses on whistleblower legal support, while Declassify UAP concentrates on legislative access and public education.

name: British UFO Research Association
description: The oldest civilian UFO investigation organization in the United Kingdom, founded in 1962 through the merger of several regional groups. BUFORA trains field investigators, maintains a substantial case database, and has built an archive of UK UAP reports spanning six decades. The organization takes an academically oriented approach with methodological guidelines informed by scientific research. Nick Pope, who ran the UK Ministry of Defence's UFO Desk (1991-1994), is an adjacent figure in the broader BUFORA network. BUFORA adds significant geographic and historical diversity to UAP research coverage.

name: Archives for the Unexplained
description: The world's largest archive dedicated to UFO/UAP and anomalous phenomena documentation, housed in Norrkoping, Sweden. Founded in 1973, AFU maintains over 50,000 items including books, journals, newspapers, correspondence, audio recordings, and video, with an ongoing digitization program making holdings internationally accessible. The archive spans the complete history of UFO research from the 1940s to the present, including rare European periodicals, investigation reports from national research groups, and personal collections of prominent researchers. AFU is an essential primary source repository complementing the Black Vault's U.S.-centric FOIA collection with European and global historical documentation.

name: Enigma Labs
description: A technology company building a crowdsourced UAP sighting data platform that applies machine learning and structured data collection to civilian reports. Founded in 2022 with venture capital backing, Enigma Labs approaches UAP data collection using modern data science practices: standardized report schemas, AI-assisted quality filtering, and systematic deduplication. The platform's emphasis on structured, analyzable data distinguishes it from earlier generation databases. Its sighting data collection function is analogous to NUFORC and MUFON but with a data-science-first methodology, and has direct relevance to DECUR's /sightings integration.

name: EuroUFO
description: A pan-European network coordinating national UAP research groups and sharing case data across borders. EuroUFO serves as a coordination layer connecting organizations including BUFORA (UK), GEIPAN (France), and national research groups across continental Europe to enable cross-border investigation of multi-national cases and to harmonize investigation standards. European UAP case coverage is significantly underrepresented in U.S.-centric research; EuroUFO represents the primary infrastructure for pan-European coordination.

name: Scientific and Medical Network
description: An international network of scientists, physicians, and researchers promoting dialogue at the frontiers of science and consciousness. Founded in 1973 in the UK, SMN supports post-materialist research and hosted the Galileo Commission initiative, which produced a landmark 2019 report signed by over 90 academics calling for expansion of science beyond the materialist paradigm. Several researchers in DECUR's network have addressed SMN events, including those at the intersection of consciousness studies and UAP phenomenology. SMN publications and dialogues frequently bridge parapsychology, neuroscience, and philosophy of mind.
